docs.title: ReghTechLab Portfolio
docs.text: Welcome to the documentation for ReghTechLab Portfolio, a full-stack web application built with Python, Flask, SQLAlchemy, and Tailwind CSS.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
The ReghTechLab portfolio is designed to showcase projects, host a fully functional blog, and manage a community of users who can interact through comments and likes.

It includes:

  • Authentication System: Secure registration and login.
  • Blog Engine: Features for creating, publishing, and interacting with posts.
  • Modern UI: Designed with Tailwind CSS for a responsive, sleek experience.

docs.title: Navigating the Docs
docs.text:
• Getting Started: Learn how to run the project locally.
  • Features: Deep dive into the user-facing capabilities of the app.
  • Architecture: Understand the app's internal database structure.

docs.title: Getting Started
docs.text: This guide will help you set up the ReghTechLab portfolio app on your local machine for development and testing.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
• Python 3.8+
  • pip (Python package installer)

docs.title: Installation
docs.text:
1. Clone the repository (if you haven't already).
  2. Create a virtual environment: 
    python -m venv venv
  3. Activate the virtual environment:
  4. Windows: venv\Scripts\activate
  5. macOS/Linux: source venv/bin/activate
  6. Install dependencies: 
    pip install -r requirements.txt

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text: The application uses an SQLite database (portfolio.db) by default. It relies on environmental variables, primarily SECRET_KEY, which defaults to a development string if not provided.

docs.title: Running the App
docs.text:
Start the Flask development server:

python app.py

The app will become available at http://127.0.0.1:5000/.

Note: The SQLite database and all necessary tables will be created automatically the first time you run app.py.

docs.title: Database Models
docs.text: The app relies on Flask-SQLAlchemy connected to an SQLite database. Below are the key data models and their relationships.

docs.title: Entity Relationship Summary
docs.text:
erDiagram
    USER ||--o{ POST : authors
    USER ||--o{ COMMENT : writes
    USER ||--o{ LIKE : gives
    POST ||--o{ COMMENT : has
    POST ||--o{ LIKE : receives

docs.title: User
docs.text:
• Fields: id, username, email, password_hash, bio, is_admin, joined_at.
  • Relationships: posts, comments, likes.

docs.title: Post
docs.text:
• Fields: id, title, slug, content, excerpt, category, published, author_id, created_at.
  • Relationships: comments, likes.
  • Properties: Includes computed properties for like_count, comment_count, and read_time.

docs.title: Comment
docs.text:
• Fields: id, content, author_id, post_id, created_at.
  • Connects a User's thought to a specific Post.

docs.title: Like
docs.text:
• Fields: id, user_id, post_id.
  • Acts as a pure junction table linking a User and Post with a UniqueConstraint on (user_id, post_id).

docs.title: Authentication System
docs.text: The application uses Flask-Login to handle user sessions and authentication.

docs.title: Features
docs.text:
• Registration: Users can sign up with a unique username, email, and password.
  • Flexible Login: Users can log in using either their username or email address.
  • Password Hashing: Passwords are securely hashed using werkzeug.security (generate_password_hash and check_password_hash) before being stored in the database.
  • Session Management: Robust session handling out-of-the-box thanks to Flask-Login (login_user, logout_user, login_required).

docs.title: Security Built-in
docs.text:
• No plain-text passwords are ever stored.
  • Secure fallback defaults are provided for the SECRET_KEY required by Flask for signing session cookies.

docs.title: Blog & Community
docs.text: The core feature of the ReghTechLab application is its robust blogging and community engagement system.

docs.title: Posts
docs.text:
• Rich Content: Posts contain titles, slugs, plain text/markdown content, and excerpts.
  • Categorization: Posts can be grouped by categories.
  • Read Time Estimation: The app dynamically calculates the estimated read time of a post based on word count (assuming ~200 words per minute).

docs.title: Engagement (Comments & Likes)
docs.text:
• Comments: Registered users can leave comments on any published post.
  • Likes: Users can "like" posts. A strict database unique constraint ensures a user can only like a specific post once.
  • Dynamic Counters: Display real-time sums for comments and likes via configured SQLAlchemy relationships and properties (like_count, comment_count).
